acts.label: Act 1 — Detection
acts.title: Something is wrong
acts.brief: It is 07:42 on a Monday morning. The IT helpdesk begins receiving calls about systems being slow or inaccessible. An alert fires in the SOC dashboard.
acts.ticker: 07:42 — Multiple users reporting encrypted files and a ransom note on screen.

nodes.title: Check the SOC alert details
nodes.result: The SIEM shows lateral movement starting at 02:14. Ransomware binary deployed across 43 endpoints in Finance and Operations.

children.title: Identify patient zero
children.result: User jsmith@corp.com opened a malicious attachment at 01:58 from a spoofed CFO email.

children.title: Check backup status
children.result: Last verified backup completed 3 days ago. Today's incremental backup was partially encrypted.

nodes.title: Review network traffic logs
nodes.result: Outbound C2 traffic detected to 185.220.x.x (known Tor exit node). Approximately 2.3 GB of data exfiltrated before encryption began.

options.feedback: Correct action. Isolation halted lateral movement but caused brief service disruption for 300 staff.
Delayed action. The ransomware spread to 2 additional departments while monitoring continued.
Good. Early escalation allows leadership to prepare crisis communications and engage legal.
Risky. Without executive awareness, key decisions were delayed by 2 hours.

acts.label: Act 2 — Escalation
acts.title: Crisis is confirmed
acts.brief: CISO has been notified. Legal, HR, and Comms teams are being assembled. The ransom note demands $2.3M in cryptocurrency within 72 hours.
acts.ticker: 09:15 — Media is asking questions. A customer portal has been offline for 90 minutes.

nodes.title: Assess scope of encrypted systems
nodes.result: Confirmed: 43 endpoints, 2 file servers, 1 ERP system (SAP), and the customer-facing web portal are encrypted. Core banking and email remain online.

children.title: Estimate recovery time without paying ransom
children.result: IT estimates 7–14 days for full recovery using backups. ERP data loss of approximately 3 days.

nodes.title: Review cyber insurance policy
nodes.result: Policy covers ransomware response up to $5M. Coverage requires notification within 24 hours of discovery. The insurer must approve any ransom payment.

options.feedback: Strong. CMT activation enables structured command, coordination, and faster decision-making.
Poor. IT-only response lacked authority to make key business and legal decisions quickly.
Correct. Timely notification preserves coverage. Insurer dispatched a forensics firm within 4 hours.
Policy breach. Late notification voided a portion of coverage, adding $400K to recovery costs.

acts.label: Act 3 — Business Impact
acts.title: Counting the cost
acts.brief: The Crisis Management Team is assembled. Finance is preparing impact figures. Customers and partners are calling for updates.
acts.ticker: 11:30 — Stock price has dropped 4%. Two enterprise clients have emailed requesting status updates.

nodes.title: Conduct Business Impact Analysis
nodes.result: Critical processes offline: Order processing, customer portal, financial reporting. Revenue impact estimated at $180K/day. SLA breach for 3 Tier-1 clients imminent.

children.title: Identify manual workarounds
children.result: Finance can process payroll manually for up to 5 days. Orders can be taken via phone with 40% reduced capacity.

nodes.title: Assess reputational risk
nodes.result: Social media monitoring shows 142 mentions, mostly concerned customers. No mainstream media coverage yet. One industry journalist has made direct contact.

options.feedback: Effective. The statement contained speculation, reassured clients, and reduced media pressure.
Costly. The silence was interpreted as cover-up. Negative media coverage began within 2 hours.
Excellent. Manual workarounds reduced operational downtime by an estimated 60%.
Missed opportunity. Two days of revenue loss could have been partially offset by BCP activation.

acts.label: Act 4 — Crisis Management
acts.title: Making the hard calls
acts.brief: Day 2. IT has begun recovery. The threat actor has published a sample of stolen data on a dark web forum as proof of exfiltration. Regulators have been informed.
acts.ticker: Day 2, 10:00 — Data leak confirmed. Legal advises potential PDPA notification obligation.

nodes.title: Assess the data exfiltration scope
nodes.result: Stolen data includes: 14,200 customer records (name, email, partial payment data), internal financial forecasts, and HR salary data. No passport or ID numbers confirmed stolen.

children.title: Check PDPA notification requirements
children.result: Under Thai PDPA, notification to PDPC required within 72 hours of confirmed breach. Customer notification required if high risk of harm.

nodes.title: Consult on ransom payment decision
nodes.result: Legal advises paying ransom carries legal risk and no guarantee. FBI recommends no payment. Insurer is neutral but will cover up to $1M if approved. Decryption tools may exist.

nodes.title: Notify affected customers about data breach?

options.feedback: Legally compliant and trust-building. Customers responded with concern but appreciated honesty.

options.text: No — disclose only if legally forced
options.feedback: High-risk strategy. Regulator discovered the breach independently and issued a formal warning.
Defensible decision. Recovery took longer but avoided legal risk and funding criminal actors.
Controversial. Recovery was faster but leaked to media, causing significant reputational damage.

acts.label: Act 5 — Recovery
acts.title: Building back
acts.brief: Day 5. Systems are being restored. Leadership is under pressure. The board wants a full incident review. Staff morale is low after 5 days of disruption.
acts.ticker: Day 5, 14:00 — 70% of systems restored. Full recovery estimated in 48 hours.

nodes.title: Verify integrity of restored systems
nodes.result: Forensic team confirms all restored systems are clean. Persistence mechanisms removed. New EDR deployed on all endpoints. Firewall rules updated.

children.title: Confirm backup restoration success
children.result: 98% of data restored. 3-day ERP gap filled manually by Finance team. No further data confirmed lost.

nodes.title: Review total business impact
nodes.result: Direct costs: $620K (forensics, overtime, tooling). Indirect costs: estimated $1.4M (lost revenue, SLA penalties, contract renegotiation). Total: ~$2M.

options.feedback: Best practice. 14 action items identified. Board received a full After-Action Report within 3 weeks.

options.text: No — move on and focus on operations
options.feedback: Missed. Without a review, root causes remain unaddressed and repeat incidents become more likely.

nodes.title: Invest in BCP and DR program improvements?

options.text: Yes — allocate budget and assign owner
options.feedback: Excellent. The board approved a 3-year resilience program. Posture significantly improved.
Short-sighted. Auditors flagged insufficient investment as a material risk in the annual review.
